title: sapium biglandulosum var. aucuparium
intro: Sapium biglandulosum var. aucuparium, also known as the Wild Lime or False Lime, is a plant species from the family Euphorbiaceae. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Sapium biglandulosum var. aucuparium include its ability to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of this plant are often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although further research is needed to fully confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
